verse_text: But I will tell you what is noted in the Scripture of Truth-and there is no one loyal to me concerning this, except Michael your prince.

links.context: The scroll contains the secret decrees of God concerning the judgment and salvation of humanity (see [[EZK.2:9]], [[EZK.2:10]]; [[PSA.138:16]]; see also 1En 81:1, 2). Included is the greatest of all mysteries, God's will concerning the events marking the end times (see [[DAN.10:21]]), sealed up and hidden in profound security (with seven seals) until the time it will be revealed and implemented. In the twenty-three references to scrolls in Revelation there are five types: (1) the unsealed letters to the churches of Asia; (2) the unsealed Book of Life (13:8ff.); (3) the unsealed books of judgment (20:12); (4) the little open scroll that is eaten (10:8-10); and (5) the present sealed scroll.

Raphael reveals himself to Tobit and Tobias as one of seven holy angels who appear before the glory of the Holy One (see [[REV.1:4]]). These seven are also called archangels. The names of four of them are revealed: Raphael, Gabriel ([[DAN.9:21]]; [[LUK.1:19]], [[LUK.1:26]]-38), Michael ([[DAN.10:21]]; Jude 9) and Uriel (2 Esdras 4:1).
